{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"SEED = 9407","old":"SEED = 42","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 2
BASE_CHANNELS = 16
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1
FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 1
BASE_CHANNELS = 8
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0
FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------

LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3

MOMENTUM = 0.9

USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"# --- LR schedule hyperparameters --------------------------------------------
USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels: int,
        out_channels: int | None = None,
        stride: int = 1,
        dropout: float = 0.0,
        use_bn: bool = True,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        out_channels = in_channels if out_channels is None else out_channels
        bias = not use_bn
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 3, stride=stride, padding=1, bias=bias)
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels) if use_bn else nn.Identity()
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(out_channels, out_channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels) if use_bn else nn.Identity()
        if stride != 1 or in_channels != out_channels:
            shortcut_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
                nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 1, stride=stride, bias=bias)
            ]
            if use_bn:
                shortcut_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
            self.shortcut = nn.Sequential(*shortcut_layers)
        else:
            self.shortcut = nn.Identity()
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = self.shortcut(x)
        out = self.activation(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.bn2(self.conv2(out))
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","old":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, channels: int, dropout: float = 0.0, use_bn: bool = True):
        super().__init__()
        bias = not use_bn
        layers1: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        layers2: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if use_bn:
            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        self.conv1 = nn.Sequential(*layers1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Sequential(*layers2)
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = x
        out = self.activation(self.conv1(x))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.conv2(out)
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, BASE_CHANNELS, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(BASE_CHANNELS))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        blocks: list[nn.Module] = []
        stage_channels = [BASE_CHANNELS * (CHANNEL_MULT**stage) for stage in range(3)]
        for stage_idx, out_channels in enumerate(stage_channels):
            stride = 1 if stage_idx == 0 else 2
            blocks.append(
                ResidualBlock(
                    channels,
                    out_channels=out_channels,
                    stride=stride,
                    dropout=DROPOUT_RATE,
                    use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM,
                )
            )
            channels = out_channels
            for _ in range(max(1, DEPTH) - 1):
                blocks.append(
                    ResidualBlock(
                        channels,
                        out_channels=channels,
                        dropout=DROPOUT_RATE,
                        use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM,
                    )
                )
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*blocks)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps), eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05)","old":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Widen and deepen the micro-ResNet, add stage-wise downsampling with projection shortcuts, and pair it with AdamW plus cosine decay over the actual step budget. This is the highest-leverage first move for a tiny CIFAR-10 model while staying simple and contract-safe.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"],"rationale":"Push the short-budget optimizer harder: remove dropout and weight decay, disable the cosine decay that may be shrinking the step size too fast, and raise AdamW's learning rate. With only 256 steps, faster early fitting is more likely to help than extra regularization.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"],"rationale":"The constant-2e-3 run overshot. Keep the low-regularization setting, but make each of the 256 updates more informative by doubling batch size, softening AdamW, and restoring cosine decay. That should improve optimization stability without bringing back the heavier regularization that limited the earlier run.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The optimizer settings are now reasonable; the remaining gap is more likely model capacity than step size. Deepen the residual stack one notch and enlarge the classifier head while keeping the tuned training setup intact.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The deeper/wider head regressed, so this is likely a capacity overreach rather than an optimization problem. Roll back to the previously successful depth and head size while keeping the better optimizer and batch/schedule settings.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 2","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Restore the earlier learning-rate schedule that was closest to the best run, and spend the extra capacity only in the classifier head where it is cheap. The deeper or heavier-head variants already regressed, so this is the safest remaining capacity tweak.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.75e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps), eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.1)","new":"return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps), eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The short fixed-step run is probably decaying too hard near the end. Keep the current architecture and optimizer, but raise the cosine floor so the last third of training still makes meaningful updates.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps), eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05)","new":"return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps), eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The backbone and schedule look stable now; the remaining lever is a larger classifier head to better use the pooled features without changing the training dynamics.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The 512-wide head overreached. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Batch 256 clearly helped, so the remaining gap is likely in how aggressively the run uses that larger-batch regime. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"Batch 256 already paid off; one more step toward a larger batch should let the same fixed update budget cover more of CIFAR-10, and the current schedule is already stable enough to support it.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The run is already spending a lot of wall time, so the next safe lever is to keep the same batch/architecture and make the late steps less conservative. Raising the cosine floor should let the model keep learning instead of tapering off too hard.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps), eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.15)","new":"return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps), eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.2)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The large-batch regime is stable and the cosine floor is already high, so the remaining no-cost lever is a slightly stronger peak learning rate to push a bit more progress out of the fixed step budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.75e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The last few changes show the run is sensitive and the latest increase overshot. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The run now looks more underfit than overfit. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["layout","caching"],"rationale":"The strongest pattern so far is simply more examples per fixed step.
